codeReview.summary: The commit correctly fixes the spikes-mcp release download base URL to the actual GitHub repository owner and keeps related metadata/tests consistent. This directly addresses the observed 404 download failure and aligns with the feature intent to validate post-release binary download and MCP startup behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker-fullstack skill lacks explicit guidance for validation-only/post-release verification tasks, which this feature required. This gap is already reflected by worker feedback and suggests the skill procedure is less aligned with this task type.

Canonical repository slug usage is inconsistent across the codebase (both bierlingm/spikes and moritzbierling/spikes), which enabled this wrapper regression and can reintroduce similar release-download failures in other surfaces.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Current repo grep still shows old slug in multiple files: action/action.yml:96, README.md:134, site/agents.md:269, cli/Cargo.toml:7, cli/src/commands/update.rs:20, action/README.md:23/91/130

summary: Pass. The feature intent is satisfied: the reviewed change points the npm wrapper to the correct release source and accompanying evidence shows post-release download + MCP initialize behavior now works. No blocking or non-blocking code defects were found in the scoped commit; two shared-state observations were recorded (skill guidance gap and repo-slug convention drift).
